title: Graphardisia bracteolata
taxonomy: Graphardisia bracteolata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical regions of the world. It belongs to the genus Graphardisia, which is closely related to the genus Xylocarpus. Graphardisia bracteolata is further classified within the family Annonaceae, a large family of flowering plants that includes over 2,000 species. The classification of Graphardisia bracteolata is based on its botanical characteristics, including its fruit and leaf morphology. Graphardisia bracteolata is considered an important species within its genus and family due to its unique characteristics and distribution.

Its roots, leaves, and bark are commonly utilized in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. In some parts of the world, it is believed to have antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. intro: Graphardisia bracteolata, also known as the Guianese taspine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Graphardisia bracteolata include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of tinctures and extracts.
